scope_note: Northwestern University does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution on the cited official pages used here. The policy section stays official; the estimate card below is third-party and directional.
policy_summary: Northwestern does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Instead, Northwestern asks counselors to contextualize a student's overall high school curriculum and invites applicants to explain circumstances that significantly affected academic performance.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Northwestern does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Instead, Northwestern asks counselors to contextualize a student's overall high school curriculum and invites applicants to explain circumstances that significantly affected academic performance.

recommended_gpa.summary: Northwestern does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class on the cited official pages. The estimate below is a third-party directional reference, not an official Northwestern cutoff.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated by PrepScholar because the university did not officially report an average incoming high-school GPA on the cited admissions pages.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: This estimate points to near-straight-A grades plus rigorous coursework. Northwestern's official materials emphasize transcript context and explanatory context over any fixed GPA bar.
